title: The No-Nonsense Guide to Building a Crypto Store on GitHub Pages — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Ethiopia, trying to sell your ebooks, courses, or art online. PayPal and Stripe don't work for you due to geographic restrictions and banking limitations. You've tried other platforms, but they either charge high fees or don't support your local currency. Your sales are dwindling, and you're losing motivation. It's time to break free from this cycle and explore alternative payment methods that can help you reach your audience without the hassle of KYC requirements or geographical restrictions. In this guide, we'll show you how to build a crypto store on GitHub Pages, no PayPal required.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Using a crypto store built on GitHub Pages offers several advantages. For one, it allows you to bypass traditional payment processors and their associated fees. You can accept payments directly from your customers, without worrying about KYC requirements or geographical restrictions. Additionally, GitHub Pages provides a secure and reliable platform for hosting your store, ensuring that your customers' transactions are processed smoothly and efficiently. By leveraging the power of cryptocurrency, you can expand your reach and increase your sales, even in the most restrictive markets.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin building your crypto store, you'll need to acquire the necessary tools and knowledge. First, you'll need to set up a GitHub account and create a new repository for your store. Next, you'll need to install the required software and dependencies, including the Full Stack Bundle ($79.99), which provides everything you need to get started, from the backend and payment layer to the Admin Dashboard and NOWPayments. Familiarize yourself with the documentation and tutorials provided, and make sure you understand the basics of cryptocurrency and smart contracts before proceeding.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

To set up your payment and delivery pipeline, you'll need to integrate the Web3 Wallet Connect and NOWPayments into your store. This will allow you to accept payments directly from your customers and automate the delivery of your digital products. You'll also need to configure the payment settings and delivery options in the Admin Dashboard. Make sure to test your payment and delivery pipeline thoroughly to ensure that everything is working correctly before going live.
Your store's security is only as strong as its weakest link, so make sure to keep your software and dependencies up to date.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and make your first sale. Create a new page for your store on GitHub Pages, and configure the settings to match your needs. Add your digital products and configure the pricing and payment options. Finally, share your store with your audience and wait for the sales to roll in. Don't forget to monitor your store's performance and adjust the settings as needed to optimize your sales and revenue.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

As your sales increase, you'll need to scale your system to handle the growing demands. This may involve upgrading your hardware or software, optimizing your payment and delivery pipeline, or adding new features to your store. Make sure to keep your system secure and reliable, and continuously monitor your performance to identify areas for improvement. With the right tools and knowledge, you can scale your crypto store and reach new heights of success.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The question on everyone's mind is: 'Is this really possible, or am I just dreaming?' The answer is simple: yes, it is possible. With the right tools and knowledge, you can build a crypto store on GitHub Pages that accepts payments directly from customers and automates the delivery of digital products. No PayPal required.
A well-optimized payment pipeline can increase your sales by up to 30%, so don't neglect this crucial step.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're interested in exploring other payment methods or scaling your crypto store, check out our related topics on GitHub Pages, including integrating cryptocurrency wallets, optimizing payment processing, and automating delivery pipelines. Consider upgrading to the Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) for a more comprehensive solution. You can also connect with our community on Discord to learn from other creators and get support when you need it.
Your Action Checklist

Before going live with your crypto store, make sure to check the following: 1. You have a GitHub account and a repository set up for your store. 2. You've installed the required software and dependencies, including the Full Stack Bundle. 3. You've integrated the Web3 Wallet Connect and NOWPayments into your store. 4. You've configured the payment settings and delivery options in the Admin Dashboard. 5. You've tested your payment and delivery pipeline thoroughly. 6. You've created a new page for your store on GitHub Pages. 7. You've added your digital products and configured the pricing and payment options.
Automating delivery pipelines can save you up to 90% of your time, so consider using a tool like Web3 Wallet Connect to streamline your processes.
Full Stack Bundle — $79.99. Everything — Core Engine, Wallet Connect, Dashboard — plus priority updates, Discord support, and deployment guide. Your complete store infrastructure, one payment.
